overview: A single lightweight backend service exposing a simple HTTP API. The service provides one GET endpoint at /hello that returns a JSON hello world message. This minimal design consists of a single microservice component deployed as a containerized application on Kubernetes.
requirements: The service must expose a GET /hello endpoint
The /hello endpoint must return a JSON response with a hello world message
The service must include a /health endpoint for readiness and liveness probes
The service must be containerized and deployable on Kubernetes
The service must listen on port 9090

components.name: hello-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: A minimal HTTP API that returns a hello world message
paths:
  /hello:
    get:
      summary: Get hello world message
      operationId: getHello
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Success
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    example: Hello, World!
                required:
                  - message
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a minimal Go HTTP service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Create two endpoints: (1) GET /hello that returns a JSON response with structure {"message": "Hello, World!"} and HTTP 200 status, (2) GET /health that returns {"status": "ok"} with HTTP 200 status. Use json.NewEncoder(w).Encode() for JSON responses. Set Content-Type header to application/json for all responses. Include proper error handling. Create a Dockerfile with a multi-stage build: use golang:1.21-alpine for building and alpine:latest for the runtime image. Copy the binary to /app/hello-api and set it as the entrypoint. Expose port 9090. The main.go file should be at the root of /hello-api directory. Use http.HandleFunc for routing. Implement graceful shutdown handling OS interrupt signals. Log all incoming requests with method and path. Return proper HTTP status codes for all responses.
